clusters.title: Cluster Management
clusters.empty: No remote clusters registered. Local cluster is always available.
clusters.col.name: Cluster Name
clusters.create.title: Register Remote Cluster
clusters.create.name: Cluster Name
clusters.setup.title: How to Prepare the Kubeconfig Secret
clusters.setup.step1: 1. Prepare the kubeconfig file for the remote cluster
clusters.setup.step1.desc: Export the target context from ~/.kube/config, or create a kubeconfig using a ServiceAccount token from the remote cluster.
clusters.setup.step3: 3. Fill in the Secret name and key in the form above, then click Register
clusters.setup.step3.desc: The controller will read the kubeconfig from the Secret and establish a connection to the remote cluster. Status shows Connected on success.
clusters.setup.sa.title: Recommended: Use ServiceAccount Token (production)
clusters.setup.sa.desc: Create a read-only ServiceAccount in the remote cluster and build the kubeconfig with its token for minimal privileges and controlled expiration.

overview.title: Kubernetes AI Diagnostic Platform
overview.subtitle: CRD-driven, AI-powered cluster analysis with auto-fix capabilities
overview.card.runs.title: Diagnostic Runs
overview.card.runs.desc: Create a DiagnosticRun CR — an AI Agent automatically scans your cluster for health, security, cost, and reliability issues
overview.card.skills.title: Skill Management
overview.card.skills.desc: 5 built-in analysis skills + custom DiagnosticSkill CRs for flexible diagnostic extensions
overview.card.fixes.title: Smart Fixes
overview.card.fixes.desc: Generate patches or new resources from findings with Before/After diff preview and human-in-the-loop approval

about.title: Architecture & Features
about.arch.title: System Architecture
about.arch.desc: The Controller watches 4 CRDs via 5 Reconcilers: creates isolated Agent Pods for diagnostics, where Claude reasons over 16 MCP tools in multi-turn loops. EventCollector runs in the background, capturing K8s Warning events and Prometheus metric snapshots into SQLite for historical context. Supports cron-based scheduled runs, Fix generation with human approval, and automatic rollback.
about.crd.title: CRD Overview (k8sai.io/v1alpha1)
about.crd.run: DiagnosticRun — Declares a diagnostic task: target namespaces, skills, timeout, output language; supports spec.schedule for cron-based scheduling + historyLimit
about.crd.skill: DiagnosticSkill — Defines analysis logic per dimension (health/security/cost/reliability): Prompt + tool whitelist, 10 built-in + custom extensible
about.crd.model: ModelConfig — LLM provider config: API Key Secret ref, model name, optional baseURL proxy, maxTurns control
about.crd.fix: DiagnosticFix — Fix proposal: strategic-merge/json-patch/create strategy, human approval flow (PendingApproval→Approved→Applying→Succeeded), auto-rollback support
about.crd.cluster: ClusterConfig — Multi-cluster management: references a Secret containing kubeconfig to register remote clusters, supports independent Prometheus URL; DiagnosticRun uses clusterRef to target a specific cluster
about.flow.title: Diagnostic & Fix Flow
about.flow.step1.desc: Via Dashboard /diagnose page or kubectl apply — one-time or scheduled (cron expression)
about.flow.step2.desc: Controller creates an isolated Agent Job (minimal-privilege ServiceAccount) — Claude makes multi-turn calls to 16 MCP tools
about.flow.step3.desc: Structured issues grouped by dimension and severity (critical→info), with zh/en output language support
about.flow.step4.desc: Click 'Generate Fix' on any finding — a short-lived Pod reads the target resource via LLM and produces a patch or new resource manifest
about.flow.step5.desc: Review Before/After diff, approve to let FixReconciler apply. Auto-rollback to snapshot on failure.
about.tools.title: MCP Tool Set (16 tools)
about.tools.desc: Agent Pod embeds k8s-mcp-server (compiled Go binary) with 16 diagnostic tools:
about.tools.list: kubectl_get · kubectl_describe · kubectl_logs · kubectl_explain · events_list · events_history · top_pods · top_nodes · list_api_resources · prometheus_query · prometheus_alerts · metric_history · kubectl_rollout_status · node_status_summary · pvc_status · network_policy_check
